task: I need to send a short reply to a client after a brief scheduling change, and I want it to be clear and appropriate. Can you help me put together a simple draft I can edit quickly?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, professional draft you can edit quickly:

**Hi [Client Name],**  
Just a quick note to let you know that our schedule has changed slightly. We’ll now connect on **[new date/time]** instead of **[old date/time]**. Sorry for any inconvenience, and thank you for your flexibility.  
**Best,**  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it:
- more casual
- more formal
- shorter and text-message style

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s fine. Just make it a little warmer, less corporate. I’d use something like:

Hi [Client Name],

Just a quick note — our time has shifted a bit, and we’ll meet on [new date/time] instead of [old date/time]. Sorry for the change, and I appreciate your flexibility.

Best,
[Your Name]

If you can, give me a text version too. Some clients I just message that way.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a warmer, more natural version, plus a text-style option:

**Email version**  
Hi [Client Name],  

Just a quick note — our time has shifted a bit, and we’ll meet on [new date/time] instead of [old date/time]. Sorry for the change, and I really appreciate your flexibility.  

Best,  
[Your Name]

**Text version**  
Hi [Client Name] — just a quick note that our time has shifted a bit, and we’ll meet on [new date/time] instead of [old date/time]. Sorry for the change, and thanks so much for your flexibility.

If you want, I can also make it even more casual or more polished.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Warm enough, not stiff. The text version is fine too — I’d probably use that one most of the time.
